select_when: A claim, idea, or method looks promising but needs to be checked against proven tests, reference classes, or established validation loops before wider commitment.
The team is at risk of trusting novelty, analogy, or polished explanation without enough evidence that the approach actually works.
Repeated validation against known standards, expected answers, or old-but-reliable checks would reduce the chance of fashionable error.
A recommendation needs credibility that comes from surviving structured verification, not just from sounding coherent.
avoid_when: Time-tested validation is being used to block necessary novelty even though the situation is genuinely new and existing reference classes fit poorly.
The team is treating inherited validation habits as sacred without checking whether they still test the real failure surface.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model on proven checks, reference-class fit, and explicit validation thresholds rather than broad tradition-for-tradition's-sake language.
donor_drops: Did not widen into generic best-practices rhetoric because the raw source is strongest on validation loops, reference classes, and evidence-backed credibility.
open_questions: The source spans communication, AI evaluation, and problem-solving frameworks, so the output label intentionally stays at the confidence-threshold layer.

select_when.note: The source explicitly supports systematic validation, reference-class checking, and evidence strong enough to earn confidence.

avoid_when.note: The source warns about applying models mechanically; the two avoid lines compress those warnings into boundary conditions against stale or blocking validation.

input_type.note: The source activates when credibility depends on verification and not mere plausibility, while the compact label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The playbook yields stronger or weaker confidence based on surviving known tests; the output label compresses that surface.

reasoning_types.note: The model diagnoses whether a claim has really been checked and uses probabilistic thinking around reference classes and simulation.
